finding: After `taskflow close --write` lands planning commits in `3KLife`, the planning task index (`docs/ai_atomic_framework/rft-hardening/tasks/README.md`) and plan snapshot can still list completed RFT cards as `planned` or in the open-priority queue until an operator manually edits and commits planning docs.
expectedBehavior: Planning close bundles should either update the index/README as part of governed closeout, or emit an explicit post-close advisory with the exact docs paths and frontmatter fields that still lag ledger reality.
evidenceOrRepro: Fixed by adding `atm.taskflowPlanningIndexAdvisory.v1` to successful `taskflow close --write` results whenever a planning roster/index path or roster follow-up command is present. The advisory reports the planning repo root, README/index path, planning card path, closeback frontmatter fields, status (`updated`, `already-current`, or `follow-up-required`), row diff, and exact `tasks roster update` command. Verification: `node --strip-types packages/cli/src/commands/taskflow/__tests__/close-residue-advisory.spec.ts` and `npm run typecheck` pass.
followUp: Keep inline roster sync in the close bundle; the advisory is the operator-facing audit trail and fallback when a profile uses follow-up roster sync.
